title: stylidium galioides
intro: Stylidium galioides, also known as the tick-tongue or tick-tongue triggerplant, is a small, annual plant native to Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian indigenous medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and treat wounds. Stylidium galioides has been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, colds, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Stylidium galioides can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
